item.title: Holland-Dozier-Holland
item.description: Era: 1962–1967 (Motown); 1968–present (independent) Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Girl group Key collaborators: The Supremes, The Four Tops, The Temptations, Martha and the Vandellas RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The most prolific and commercially successful songwriting-production team in the history of Mot...

item.title: Jeff Barry
item.description: Era: 1960s–1970s (primary period: 1961–1969) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll, bubblegum Key collaborator: Ellie Greenwich (co-writer and wife, 1962–1965) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Jeff Barry was the Brill Building’s most instinctive hitmaker — the songwriter who understood, more clea...

item.title: Stevie Wonder
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, funk, art pop Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1971–1976) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The riff that opens “Superstition” — that dirty, stacked Hohner Clavinet line — Stevie Wonder built alone, laying down the drums first and then overdubbing ...

item.title: The Four Tops
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1953–present (classic era 1964–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Levi Stubbs had a voice built for crisis — a baritone-tenor that could move from conversational warmth to desperate, shouting intensity with an abruptness that made every song...

item.title: The Ronettes
item.description: Genres: Girl group, Brill Building, Pop soul, Rhythm & blues Era: 1961–1967 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Ronettes are the girl group at its most potent, the act that proved what happened when Brill Building craft and Phil Spector’s Wall of Sound met a voice strong enough to ride them both.

item.title: (1963) "Be My Baby"
item.description: The RonettesReleasedAugust 1963LabelPhilles RecordsProducerPhil SpectorStudioGold Star StudiosSongwritersJeff Barry, Ellie Greenwich, Phil SpectorGenresPrimaryGirl groupBrill BuildingSecondaryPop soulSoul jazzRhythm & bluesListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The most perfect pop single ever recorded, ...

item.title: (1964) "Leader of the Pack"
item.description: The Shangri-LasReleasedSeptember 1964LabelRed Bird RecordsProducerGeorge “Shadow” MortonSongwritersGeorge “Shadow” Morton, Jeff Barry, Ellie GreenwichGenresPrimaryGirl groupBrill BuildingSecondaryTeen popTraditional popListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify It opens with a single piano chord struck like a to...

item.title: Gerry Goffin
item.description: Era: 1960s–1990s (primary period: 1960–1967) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll, soul Key collaborator: Carole King (co-writer and wife, 1959–1968) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Gerry Goffin was the lyricist of the most prolific and versatile songwriting team in the Brill Building — the par...
